# RING++: Roto-Translation Invariant Gram for Global Localization on a Sparse Scan Map

- **Work ID:** `LPR-024`
- **Reviewed version:** `arxiv:2210.05984`
- **Year / venue:** 2023 / TRO
- **Relevance:** `core_lpr`
- **Representative tier:** `ordinary`
- **Links:** [Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.05984)

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** NCLT, MulRan, Oxford Radar RobotCar, legged-robot multi-robot SLAM dataset — `source_fact`; manual, p. 9–12, § Abstract;III-IV. RING++;V-VI. Evaluation, Fig. 2-15;Table I-VII
- **input_unit:** single ground-removed query scan and a sparse database of map scans, each cropped to a 140 m square region — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–8, § Abstract;III-IV. RING++;V-VI. Evaluation, Fig. 2-15;Table I-VII
- **invariances:** yaw rotation and planar translation in theory, including their combination, practical translation invariance degrades with finite scan range, occlusion and discretization — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 5–9, § Abstract;III-IV. RING++;V-VI. Evaluation, Fig. 2-15;Table I-VII
- **representation:** six-channel local-geometric BEV features, Radon sinogram, row-wise DFT-magnitude translation-invariant and rotation-equivariant TING, cross-correlation and max-pooled roto-translation-invariant RING, global yaw and planar-translation solvers, ICP 6-DoF refinement — `source_fact`; manual, p. 4–9, § Abstract;III-IV. RING++;V-VI. Evaluation, Fig. 2-15;Table I-VII
- **uses_learning:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–17, § Abstract;III-IV. RING++;V-VI. Evaluation, Fig. 2-15;Table I-VII
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–17, § Abstract;III-IV. RING++;V-VI. Evaluation, Fig. 2-15;Table I-VII
